title: dalbergaria crassa
parts: Dalbergaria crassa has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine. The roots of Dalbergaria crassa contain medicinal constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of the plant are rich in terpenoids and phenolic compounds, which are used to treat various health issues, including fever and skin infections. The flowers of Dalbergaria crassa are a source of anthraquinones, which are used to treat digestive problems and relieve constipation.
side_effects: Dalbergaria crassa can have side effects if used improperly, such as increased risk of liver damage, allergic reactions, and skin irritation due to its toxic compounds. Some individuals may experience symptoms like dizziness, nausea, and headaches after consuming or coming into contact with the plant. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also lead to neurological problems, including seizures and tremors in severe cases. It's essential to handle and use Dalbergaria crassa with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional or an experienced practitioner.
